Today Klarna announces an $800m financing round during the worst stock downturn and challenging macro in decades. We are not immune to public peers being down 75-90% and hence our valuation is down on par. Here are some facts that the media might omit in reporting on this 🧵 👇

Klarna press release: our valuation is up $4.6 B, or 219%, since 2018, better than our competitors. Unstated in Klarna press release: Klarna raised roughly $3.5 B in additional cash since 2018 (per pitchbook) https://www.klarna.com/... https://twitter.com/...
Tell me you've just completed a massive down round without telling me..... https://www.klarna.com/... https://twitter.com/...

Klarna's CEO defends the company as its latest funding round values it at $6.7 billion which is an 85% drop from its previous valuation of $45.6 billion. I'm now really curious about what an updated valuation would be for Stripe given every major fintech app's decline in value. https://twitter.com/...
The entire payment industry is a bubble.. some fold and some lose 95% valuation in a year and yet are able to take almost a billion dollar in new money SMH https://twitter.com/...
And Canadian pension funds right in the thick of things, of course. https://twitter.com/...

“Klarna is no longer Europe's most valuable private fintech, with the hire-purchase 3.0 provider's latest funding round arriving at a $6.7bn pre-money valuation versus $46bn just over a year ago.” - @BryceElder blog here for @ftalphaville https://www.ft.com/...

NO Founder should think nor accept that this is a proxy for his/her company...It's just the result of arrogance. Did not need to be like this & this is the real lesson for start-up founders. @Klarna's valuation slashed massively.... https://www.ft.com/...
It's official: Klarna confirms it has raised $800m in new financing at a $6.7bn valuation - down a whopping 85% from last year https://www.klarna.com/...

Klarna raised more cash than expected ($800 million in a pure equity round) but at a (really) painfully low valuation: $5.9 billion pre-money; down from $45.6 billion in rather recent better times for the BNPL company. A serious downturn victim - for now https://tech.eu/... https://twitter.com/...

The source of the #Uberfiles has come forward to explain why he leaked 124,000 documents to the Guardian. He is Mark MacGann, formerly Uber's chief lobbyist in Europe, the Middle East and Africa. Here's his exclusive interview with us. https://www.theguardian.com/ ...

We've been leaked a trove of more than 124,000 confidential files from the tech giant Uber. If you have the app on your phone, you might want to discover the methods that enabled its global expansion.... https://www.theguardian.com/ ...

So... there is plenty of actually damning stuff in The Uber Files, so this isn't an attempt to minimize it. But so many people are focused on the “we're pirates” quote which I think means a lot less than people think... https://www.theguardian.com/ ... https://twitter.com/...

in the #UberFiles trove of 124k documents, one text message stands out. as Uber drivers were at risk of attacks by taxi drivers, former CEO Travis Kalanick assured colleagues that Uber may benefit from such violence. story from @byaaroncdavis https://www.washingtonpost.com/ ... https://twitter.com/...

Uber's public, avowed strategy was to launch where the service was ~illegal and bully politicians into approving it, rather than lobbying first, on the theory that lobbying wold fail unless you'd already shown people the service. https://www.theguardian.com/ ...

Uber's anxiety over government raids manifested in a Dawn Raid Manual that ran more than 2,600 words with 66 bullet points, which instructed: “Move the Regulators into a meeting room that does not contain any files” and “Never leave the Regulators alone.” https://www.washingtonpost.com/ ...

In an explosive new leak, the #UberFiles reveal untold forces that drove the tech company to global dominance — reshaping livelihoods, labor markets and daily transit for people around the world. From ICIJ, @guardian & 180+ journalists in 29 countries. 🧵 https://www.icij.org/...

Leaked confidential files reveal how @Uber flouted laws, duped police, exploited violence against drivers and secretly lobbied governments during its aggressive global expansion, reports the @guardian. https://www.theguardian.com/ ...
Holy moly! Honestly I don't know how Uber ever wants to be taken seriously again as a proper company that follows the law after these revelations. #UberFiles https://www.theguardian.com/ ...

“Uber executives also sought to deflect inquiries about the company's aggressive tax avoidance strategies by volunteering to help host countries collect income taxes owed by drivers, documents show.” @CICTAR_Tax https://twitter.com/...
The leak contains personal text messages between Emmanuel Macron and Uber execs, revealing how he secretly helped the Silicon Valley's lobbying operation in France. https://www.theguardian.com/ ...

Proud to have contributed to #UberFiles as they revealed not only the systemic proximity between corporate & political power but also the embarrassing inadequacy of our legal and political culture to address the former. More on @BBCPanorama tomorrow https://www.bbc.com/...

@btaylor Doesn't seem to understand what happens next. This is @elonmusk 's moment where he has Twitter's board over a barrel. Through discovery, Musk's legal team will now be able to review all internal documents regarding Twitter's bot policy. He will now see exactly how https://twitter.com/...

NEW: #UberFiles reveals that the company quietly sought alliances with billionaires and political figures close to Russian President Vladimir Putin, courting their favor through appeals from former U.S. and U.K. officials and lucrative investment deals. https://www.icij.org/...

Uber bosses also used a ‘kill switch’ during raids to stop police seeing data. The leak reveals it was deployed France, the Netherlands, Belgium, India, Hungary and Romania. https://www.theguardian.com/ ...

The European Commission is set for a fresh uproar over lobbying and revolving doors as internal Uber documents reveal the role of former Dutch Commissioner Neelie Kroes in lobbying for Uber before she joined the company. Uber employees were instructed to keep it a secret: https://twitter.com/...
Uber has acted since Day One as if it did not have to obey laws. When law breaking is your first principle, there is no governor on behavior. https://twitter.com/...
“We're just fucking illegal.” A lesson in what not to put in an email, especially if you're the global head of communications. #uberfiles https://www.theguardian.com/ ...

Uber-rooted in exploitation, w no regard to laws, common good, exploiting drivers & communities. This leak of files analyzed & reported by a world collaborative of journalists is a triumph & deeply important as we move forward through the morass these companies have created. /1 https://twitter.com/...
an intimate tour of the corporate lobbying/ political influence interface via a large Uber document leak. Props to @guardian team for the work https://twitter.com/...

The Uber Files provide the most extensive, behind-the-scenes account of how Uber executives ordered the deployment of previously reported tech tools such as the kill switch and Greyball to gain advantages over authorities. https://www.washingtonpost.com/ ...

Uber's then-chief executive, Travis Kalanick, had ordered the computer systems in Amsterdam cut off from Uber's internal network, making data inaccessible to authorities as they raided its European headquarters, documents show. https://www.washingtonpost.com/ ... https://twitter.com/...

Uber broked laws, duped police to stop them seeing data, exploited violence against drivers and secretly lobbied governments during its aggressive global expansion: •Attempts to lobby Biden, Scholz, Osborne •Macron secretly aided Uber lobbying in France https://www.theguardian.com/ ...

I don't agree with taxi drivers about most things, but they are absolutely right when they talk about how damaging Uber is for our cities. Glad to see it getting the coverage it deserves. https://twitter.com/...
What's kind of sad is that they were right to do so. Asking permission would never have worked. Disruption is disruptive. https://twitter.com/...

This confirms years of reporting that Uber operated like the mafia to create regulatory environments that were conducive to its functioning. And it's still meaningfully profitable!!! https://twitter.com/...

This is a big one. Makes Macron look bad, Scholz look good. With so many of the key leaked documents being texts & WhatsApp messages, this trove illustrates why the European Commission's insistence to rule them out as documents is such a blow for transparency & accountability. https://twitter.com/...

To me the story about Kalanick era Uber these days is that a legal reckoning never really came. Justice Department hasn't seemed to focus on anything besides the hack (which I first reported). Uber wasn't held accountable for much because government didn't do much
Great story. Would like to note that I wrote about Uber's “kill switch” back in January 2018 with @OliviaZaleski for @business https://www.bloomberg.com/... https://twitter.com/...

Uber executives activated a so-called kill switch to cut access to company servers and prevent authorities from seizing evidence during raids on company offices in at least six countries and Hong Kong, according to the leaked documents and public records. https://www.irishtimes.com/...

“Few people turned down Plouffe when he called in favours. It was just one example of how Uber leveraged Plouffe's reputation and his access to the Obama network to promote its agenda across Europe and the Middle East.” https://www.theguardian.com/ ...

“The emails in the leak also suggest Plouffe must have known about some of Uber's more dubious tactics. The files show he was included in correspondence that showed the company seeking to impede investigations or enforcement against its operations.” https://www.theguardian.com/ ...

Leaked Uber documents show how former Obama administration officials David Plouffe and Jim Messina used their connections to help Uber expand across the world, even contacting US ambassadors to help the company in countries like France and the Netherlands. https://www.theguardian.com/ ...
“Uber sought access to leaders, officials and diplomats through David Plouffe and Jim Messina, leak shows” https://www.theguardian.com/ ...
(And no, replyguys, I have no dog in this fight. My old employer a16z invested in Lyft, which was on the other side of that strategy and indeed benefited from the anti-Uber backlash)
And Travis's brand was to be assholes-as-a-strategy. He wrote ‘always be toe-stepping’ into the mission statement.

Kalanick dismissed concerns from other executives that sending Uber drivers to a protest in France put them at risk of violence from angry opponents in the taxi industry. “I think it's worth it,” he shot back. “Violence guarantee[s] success.” https://www.theguardian.com/ ...
Leak of ⁦@Uber⁩ files will be very tricky for ⁦@EU_Commission⁩ and ⁦@NeelieKroesEU⁩ https://www.bbc.com/...

“The leak also contains texts between Kalanick & Emmanuel Macron ... Macron, the French president, appears to have gone to extraordinary lengths to help Uber, even telling the company he had brokered a secret ‘deal’ with its opponents in the French cabinet” https://www.theguardian.com/ ...

“We're just fucking illegal.” A leak of >124,000 Uber documents from 2013-2017 provides an unprecedented look into the ways the company “flouted laws, duped police, exploited violence against drivers and secretly lobbied governments” in pursuit of growth. https://www.theguardian.com/ ...
So much Axe Capital energy for Uber to have once deemed it necessary to compile a “Dawn Raid Manual.” https://twitter.com/...

Uber's access extended to the highest echelons of political power. #UberFiles detail confidential meetings with presidents, prime ministers, oligarchs and more, winning the startup prominent allies like Emmanuel Macron, then France's economy minister. https://www.icij.org/... https://twitter.com/...

Live events startup Hopin, valued at $7.75B in August 2021, is laying off 242 employees, 29% of its staff, after cutting 12% of its staff in February 2022

Europe's ‘fastest growing startup ever’ seems to be deflating at pace. But founder Johnny Boufarhat can always fall back on the $195m he cashed in on the way up: https://www.ft.com/... https://twitter.com/...

One would think a virtual events platform would be stronger during a financial downturn. Enterprises are heavily scrutinizing travel budgets and leaning into virtual offerings. The issues Hopin is facing are not macroeconomic. https://twitter.com/...

Exc: Events startup Hopin is laying off 242 staff or 29% of its global workforce, confirming our earlier reporting. Valued at $7.75bn, this is the company's second set of job cuts in 2022 as it looks to build a sustainable biz. More on @BusinessInsider: https://www.businessinsider.com/ ...

Breaking: Binance Evaded US Sanctions To Serve Iranian Crypto Traders
Binance deliberately skirted US sanctions to serve Iran users, says Reuters

Exclusive: Ali, a crypto trader in Iran, said he used Binance for around a year. He is one of seven traders in Iran who spoke with Reuters about how they continued to use their Binance accounts despite U.S. sanctions and a company ban https://www.reuters.com/...

confused about this article. the headline seems to suggest that binance violated sanctions, but the lawyers cited say that they didn't bc it's not a US company https://www.reuters.com/... https://twitter.com/...
Presumptive @elonmusk/Twitter financier Binance involved in sanction-busting https://www.reuters.com/...
How North Korean spies infiltrate US crypto firms, posing as remote workers; UN says the country has stolen billions of dollars' worth of crypto from exchanges

New —> US officials say 1000s of North Koreans are landing jobs at tech firms overseas, offering a critical source of funding for DPRK weapons programs. I spoke to 1 crypto entrepreneur who, the FBI told him, had a North Korean on the payroll for months. https://www.cnn.com/...

It is wrong to say that cryptocurrency has entirely useless “in real life”. The organised criminal community has had a whale of a time, and one such group, which happens to control #North_Korea, has done especially well out of it. https://cnn.com/...

Besides hacking crypto exchanges for billions in digital funny money, North Korea has placed operatives in crypto and fintech jobs in the west. I really do wonder how many foreign intel officers (and not just adversaries) work at U.S. tech companies. https://www.cnn.com/...
“North Korean tech workers can earn more than $300,000 annually... and up to 90% of their wages go to the regime” https://twitter.com/...
Sources: defense contractor L3Harris scuttled plans to buy NSO Group despite alleged US intel officials' support, after the Biden admin's outrage over the talks
US defence firm ends talks to buy NSO Group's surveillance technology
Efforts to Acquire Pegasus Spyware's Company Backed by US Spies, Says Stingray Maker
US military contractor moves to buy Israeli spy-tech company NSO Group
American firm drops bid for Israeli spyware following U.S. concerns
U.S. Intel Officials Backed NSO Purchase, NYT Reports, but Talks Called Off
L3Harris reportedly ends talks to acquire controversial Israeli spyware maker NSO Group

There is a bit of a mystery at the heart of this story. Reporting indicates that L3 Harris believed it had the support of the US intel community as it pursued talks. But US official saying it did not. https://www.theguardian.com/ ...

The Israeli spying software Pegasus is so powerful and dangerous that the US put its maker on a black list after it was reported it was sold to “human-rights abusers.” NYT reports today: the Pentagon backed attempts by a US contractor to buy the company. https://www.nytimes.com/...

100/. #PegasusSpyware is so powerful & dangerous that the US put NSO on a black list after it was reported it was sold to “human-rights abusers” Today the @NYTimes reports that the Pentagon backed attempts by a US contractor to buy the company #Pegasus https://www.nytimes.com/...

Much of the intelligence industry's consolidation is due in part to suggestions like this from the spymasters. But this is quite an unusual case, involving L3-Harris and Israel's NRO. “Firm Said US Spies Backed Its Bid for Pegasus Spyware Maker.” (Free). https://www.nytimes.com/...

We need more “definitive pushback” against mercenary spyware firms, just like this below 👇 “White House opposition on security grounds seen as fatal obstacle to L3 Harris proceeding with purchase” of NSO Group @skirchy https://www.theguardian.com/ ...

“One of the conditions was that NSO's arsenal of ‘zero days’—vulnerabilities in computer source code that allow Pegasus to hack into mobile phones—could be sold to all of America's partners in the so-called Five Eyes intelligence sharing relationship.” https://www.nytimes.com/...
Battles over buying the world's strongest commercial intelligence technologies ↘️ https://www.nytimes.com/...

6/ #NSOGroup certainly knew a failed deal story was inbound. Of course, looks bad to their investors. To the the rest of the world...like a bit of a death spiral. Perhaps this explains some of their recent puzzling moves?

WHOA: Deal for 🇺🇸 defense contractor @L3HarrisTech to acquire #NSOGroup tanked. Counterintelligence problems reportedly played big role. Thoughts: Well, $LHX exit helps explain recent signs of desperation from the spyware company. 1/🧵 By @skirchy https://www.theguardian.com/ ... https://twitter.com/...

One of the conditions was that NSO's arsenal of “zero days” — the vulnerabilities in computer source code that allow Pegasus to hack into mobile phones — could be sold to all members of the Five Eyes: US, UK, Canada, Australia and New Zealand.

The American contractor L3 Harris is said to have cited support from intelligence officials for its effort to acquire NSO, the Israeli spyware company blacklisted by the Biden administration. https://www.nytimes.com/...

“But five people familiar with the negotiations said that the L3Harris team had brought with them a surprising message that...American intelligence officials, they said, quietly supported its plans to purchase NSO...” https://www.nytimes.com/...

A team of executives from an American military contractor quietly visited Israel numerous times in recent months to try to carry out a bold but risky purchase. They told the Israelis that U.S. intelligence supported the acquisition. W\@MarkMazzettiNYT https://www.nytimes.com/...

Sources: Myanmar's junta rolls out Chinese surveillance cameras with facial recognition tech in five more cities and plans to add them to every state in Myanmar
Myanmar's Junta Rolls Out Chinese Camera Surveillance Systems in More Cities

My story on how the Myanmar junta is ramping up surveillance systems across the country, many of which with facial recognition tech, and using cameras from Dahua, Huawei, and Hikvision. Thread with reporting not in story: 1/x https://www.reuters.com/...

Very worrisome development in #Burma with increased “surveillance cameras [that] pose a serious risk to #Myanmar's democracy activists because the military and police can use them to track their movements”, figure out networks, safehouses, vehicles: @hrw https://www.reuters.com/... https://twitter.com/...

In the state of Rakhine, where the military is battling an ethnic armed group, CCTV systems with Huawei cameras have been deployed by Myanmar's security forces since 2019 in the city of Sittwe and some villages, source said. Reported by ⁦@f_potkin⁩ https://www.reuters.com/...

What is going on with the Digital Coup/Dictatorship in #Myanmar 👇 Myanmar's junta is installing Chinese-built cameras with facial recognition capabilities in more cities across the country, three people with direct knowledge of the matter said. https://www.reuters.com/...

#Myanmar junta installing Chinese-built cameras w/ facial recognition in more cities that pose serious risks for ppl opposing the junta. This is another step toward surveillance state, helping military connect links btw networks & track movements https://www.reuters.com/... @f_potkin
VC funding in Latin America, which hit a record $14.8B in 2021 according to PitchBook, has slowed down in 2022 as investors without large portfolios pull back
We are closing our 7th LATAM investment right now. Despite the downturn, I have no doubt 2022 will be an amazing vintage, especially for this region. https://twitter.com/...

When @snyatta used to tell people he was moving to Miami to invest in LatAm startups, they thought he was crazy. Now, it's not far from the norm. Last year, venture investing exploded in the region. The question now is who is sticking around in a downturn https://www.protocol.com/...
Here to stay, not just to visit! In a very uncertain and volatile world, Miami and LatAm are deeply inspiring and energizing https://twitter.com/...
